parameters.name: sponser
parameters.description: Account ID of the sponsor. Every account in the response will either be sponsored by the given account ID or have a subentry (trustline, offer, or data entry) which is sponsored by the given account ID.

parameters.name: seller
parameters.description: The account ID of the offer creator.

parameters.name: selling_asset_type
parameters.description: The type for the selling asset. Either native, credit_alphanum4, or credit_alphanum12.

parameters.name: selling_asset_issuer
parameters.description: The Stellar address of the issuer of the asset being sold (base asset). Required if the `selling_asset_type` is not `native`.

parameters.name: selling_asset_code
parameters.description: The code for the asset being sold (base asset). Required if the `selling_asset_type` is not `native`.

parameters.name: buying_asset_type
parameters.description: The type for the asset being bought (counter asset). Either `native`, `credit_alphanum4`, or `credit_alphanum12`.

parameters.name: buying_asset_issuer
parameters.description: The Stellar address of the issuer of the asset being bought (counter asset). Required if the `buying_asset_type` is not `native`.

parameters.name: buying_asset_code
parameters.description: The code for the asset being bought (counter asset). Required if the `buying_asset_type` is not `native`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: A number that points to a specific location in a collection of responses and is pulled from the paging_token value of a record.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: A designation of the order in which records should appear. Options include `asc` (ascending) or `desc` (descending). If this argument isn’t set, it defaults to `asc`.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of records returned. The limit can range from 1 to 200 - an upper limit that is hardcoded in Horizon for performance reasons. If this argument isn’t designated, it defaults to 10.
